pi_items.source: Verified against verifier.py default (line 42) and consistent across task.json description, setup_task.sh database queries, and export_result.sh. Follows standard naming conventions for given names.

Verified against verifier.py default (line 43) and consistent across all task files (task.json description, setup_task.sh cleanup queries, export_result.sh database queries). Follows standard naming conventions for surnames.

Verified against verifier.py default (line 44). Username follows standard naming convention: first letter of first name ('M' from Margaret) + last name in lowercase ('chen'). Consistently used in all scripts (setup_task.sh line 27, export_result.sh line 30, verifier.py line 85).

Verified against verifier.py default (line 45). Email follows RFC 5322 standard format (firstname.lastname@domain). 'school.edu' is a valid educational domain suffix. Consistent with metadata first and last names. Used in task.json description and verified by verifier.py line 94.

Verified against OpenSIS documentation which lists 'Teacher' as a valid user profile/role type in OpenSIS staff management. Consistent in task.json description and verified by verifier.py lines 101-104 which checks if profile_name.lower() contains 'teacher'. OpenSIS supports three base user profiles: admin, teacher, and parent.

Used in export_result.sh line 46 to verify staff_school_relationship. Appears to be the default/primary school ID in the test environment (school_id=1 for linking staff to schools). Standard database convention where IDs start at 1.

privileged_info_summary: The task 'add_teacher_staff@1' requires adding a new staff member named Margaret Chen (username: mchen, email: margaret.chen@school.edu) with the Teacher profile and linking them to school ID 1 in OpenSIS v9.2. All metadata values are verified to be internally consistent, follow standard naming conventions (firstname-based username, proper email format), and match the expectations defined in the verifier code. The data is synthetic test data created for this automation task. The OpenSIS system confirms 'Teacher' is a valid user profile type for staff members.
